summary: rc.18 Protocol v2 — single hard-cut migration of the `plan-context-hint` JSON wire contract: bump `version: 1 → 2`, rename `payload.narrow → payload.entries` across the emitter and both .cjs consumers, update test fixtures in lockstep, and ship SOLO as the largest blast-radius change in the post-grill backlog. Pre-user clean-slate: NO v1 compat shim, NO accept-both. Consumers receiving v1 silent-skip with one stderr breadcrumb line (preserves the existing hook silent-exit-0 contract).
approach: 6-task chain anchored by an upfront decision-record commit (TASK-001) that locks two protocol-shaping choices — field name (`entries`) and v1-receipt stance (silent-skip + breadcrumb) — so the 3 parallel-safe implementation tasks (TASK-002 emitter, TASK-003 narrow consumer, TASK-004 broad consumer) read from a single source-of-truth without race conditions. TASK-005 lockstep-migrates both test files AFTER all 3 production-code tasks land (acts as the integration GREEN signal). TASK-006 cuts the release with CHANGELOG + version bump + ONE batched Gemini review + coverage check (per memory/feedback_review_batching.md — no per-task reviews). Every task = one git commit per memory/project_grill_deferred_items.md; rc.18 ships SOLO and does NOT bundle with rc.16/rc.17.
estimated_time: ~2h45m (decision 30m + emitter 30m + each consumer 30m + tests 45m + release 30m); 3 parallel-safe tasks (TASK-002/003/004) can compress wall-clock to ~2h00m if executed concurrently

design_decisions.rationale: The broad consumer (knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:443) already does a local rebind `const entries = payload.narrow ...` because the maintainer found `narrow` misleading at the rendering layer (see comment 437-441). Promoting the rebind name to the wire eliminates the local-vs-wire divergence that the existing comment explicitly flags as a deferred independent task.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Loses the historical `narrow` term (which carried path-mode connotation); gains mode-agnostic semantics (`entries` correctly describes both --paths union and --all degenerate modes). Rejected alternatives: `matchedKnowledge` (path-mode bias), `relevantKnowledge` (verbose, same bias).

design_decisions.decision: v1-receipt stance = silent-skip + one-line stderr breadcrumb
design_decisions.rationale: Hooks already wrap everything in try/catch with silent-exit-0 (broad.cjs:464 comment makes this explicit). Silent-skip is the established failure mode — version mismatch should not be the one exception that breaks the rule. The stderr breadcrumb gives debug signal without crashing SessionStart hooks during pre-user upgrade windows.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Loses loud-failure-on-drift signal (a hard-throw stance would surface mismatches immediately); gains zero-crash upgrade path. Pre-user clean-slate makes the upgrade-safety argument theoretical (no installed users to break) but the precedent matters for post-launch.

design_decisions.decision: TASK-005 (tests) deferred to after all 3 production-code tasks land — single integration commit
design_decisions.rationale: Bundling broad+narrow test migration in one commit guarantees the suite is either red or green, never partially passing. The new v1-receipt tests are the convergence proof for TASK-001's stance decision — without them the stance is documented but not enforced.
design_decisions.tradeoff: CI is red between TASK-002/003/004 commits and TASK-005 — accepted because rc.18 ships SOLO and the chain is local until merge.

design_decisions.decision: Single batched Gemini review at TASK-006, not per-task
design_decisions.rationale: Per memory/feedback_review_batching.md — for multi-task lite-plan chains, run Gemini review + coverage ONCE at end. Avoids review-fatigue and lets the reviewer see field-name consistency across emitter↔consumers↔tests in a single pass.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Drift detected late costs more to fix; mitigated by the per-task convergence criteria already grep-checking `payload.narrow` removal.

_metadata.dag_summary: TASK-001 → {TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004 in parallel} → TASK-005 → TASK-006

outstanding_clarifications.question: Veto field name `entries`?
outstanding_clarifications.veto_impact_radius: Re-runs TASK-002/003/004/005 with the chosen alternative substituted; no DAG change
outstanding_clarifications.rationale_summary: Matches existing local rebind in broad.cjs:443; mode-agnostic (covers --paths AND --all); resolves the comment block at lines 437-441 that flags this exact rename as a deferred task

outstanding_clarifications.question: Veto v1-receipt stance `silent-skip + breadcrumb`?
outstanding_clarifications.veto_impact_radius: Re-runs TASK-003/004/005 with the chosen alternative; emitter (TASK-002) unaffected
outstanding_clarifications.rationale_summary: Aligns with existing hook silent-exit-0 contract (broad.cjs:464); preserves SessionStart non-fatal property during upgrade windows

cross_phase_constraints: Single batched Gemini review + coverage at TASK-006 (memory/feedback_review_batching.md)
rc.18 ships SOLO — do NOT bundle with rc.16/rc.17
anti_scope: Do NOT add CLI flags / change command tree (rc.15 surface contract-locked)
Do NOT backport v1 compat (zero-user clean-slate)
Do NOT touch banner i18n (rc.16 territory)
Do NOT touch help text / target chain / serve warnings (rc.17 territory)
Do NOT add new payload fields beyond the rename (scope creep)

quality_check.reason: Per memory/feedback_review_batching.md — quality review batched into TASK-006 instead of per-task or per-plan-generation. Pre-execution validation handled by per-task convergence.criteria (every task has 7-11 quantified, testable criteria including grep gates and pnpm vitest commands).
